task_completion.description: Agent visited fda.gov to locate official FDA guidance on SaMD or AI/ML.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing Firefox open to an fda.gov page related to Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) or AI/ML guidance.

task_completion.description: Agent visited federalregister.gov to identify a relevant rule or notice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing Firefox open to federalregister.gov displaying a rule, notice, or search results related to medical devices or SaMD.

task_completion.description: Agent visited imdrf.org to find IMDRF guidance on SaMD classification or clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing Firefox open to imdrf.org displaying SaMD guidance documents (e.g., N10 or N41).

task_completion.description: Agent created a Firefox bookmark folder named 'FDA Regulatory Research'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox Bookmarks menu, sidebar, or Library window showing a folder explicitly named 'FDA Regulatory Research'.

task_completion.description: Agent saved at least 6 bookmarks from the required sources into the folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the 'FDA Regulatory Research' bookmark folder showing 6 or more saved web pages from FDA, Federal Register, and IMDRF.

task_completion.description: Agent created the regulatory requirements matrix file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager showing a file named 'samd_regulatory_matrix.txt' located in the ~/Documents/ directory.

task_completion.description: The matrix details FDA pre-market requirements, IMDRF SaMD classification framework, and international harmonization notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The open 'samd_regulatory_matrix.txt' file displaying text or a matrix/table that includes information on FDA pre-market requirements, IMDRF classification, and international harmonization.

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox web browser to conduct the research.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Firefox browser interface being used to navigate to the required domains, rather than using terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: Bookmarks were created using Firefox's native bookmarking functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Firefox bookmark star icon, bookmark manager, or bookmark menus, rather than just writing a list of URLs in a text file.

integrity.description: The agent compiled the matrix based on actual research rather than hallucinating the content upfront.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the agent visiting the regulatory websites before or while writing the content of the samd_regulatory_matrix.txt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Required domains are fda.gov, federalregister.gov, and imdrf.org. Required keywords in the matrix include SaMD, FDA, and IMDRF. The output file must be located exactly at /home/ga/Documents/samd_regulatory_matrix.txt. The bookmark folder must be named 'FDA Regulatory Research' and contain at least 6 bookmarks. SaMD stands for Software as a Medical Device.
